category: Descriptor Query Capability Contracts
description: Add first-class descriptor query capability metadata to the compiled MappingSet, separate from the existing root-table RelationalQueryCapability. Include supported and omitted states, supported descriptor fields, stable omission diagnostics, and a lookup helper such as GetDescriptorQueryCapabilityOrThrow. Acceptance: descriptor resources can be resolved through descriptor-specific capability metadata; non-descriptor RelationalQueryCapability behavior remains unchanged; descriptor resources still remain intentionally omitted from the generic root-table query path; omitted descriptor capabilities produce an actionable message suitable for HTTP 501 responses; missing descriptor capability metadata for a SharedDescriptorTable resource is treated as a deterministic internal guard-rail failure.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for descriptor query capability lookup, missing metadata guard rails, supported vs omitted state, and omitted-resource diagnostics.

category: Descriptor Query Capability Compilation and Manifest
description: Compile descriptor query capability entries during MappingSet compilation from ConcreteResourceModel.DescriptorMetadata.ColumnContract and expose them in mapping-set contract/manifest output. Support descriptor query fields id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate. Treat DescriptorMetadata.ColumnContract as authoritative and add the cheap ApiSchema queryFieldMapping sanity check: if the schema disagrees for a descriptor resource, mark only that descriptor capability omitted with a clear diagnostic instead of failing the whole MappingSet. Acceptance: every SharedDescriptorTable resource receives descriptor query capability metadata; query parameter names are case-insensitive; supported field-to-column mappings are deterministic; generic RelationalQueryCapability still reports descriptor resources as omitted with the existing descriptor-path reason; mapping-set manifests/goldens expose descriptor query capability diagnostics without changing non-descriptor query capability output. If MappingSet mapping-pack payload serialization/validation is active by implementation time, descriptor query capability metadata is included in that payload; if MappingSet.FromPayload remains intentionally unimplemented, mapping-pack payload changes are explicitly documented as out of scope for DMS-994.
steps-to-verify: Add compiler tests covering supported descriptor fields, case-insensitive query field names, resource-scoped omission on ApiSchema mismatch, and continued generic query omission for SharedDescriptorTable resources.
Update mapping-set manifest/golden tests to include descriptor query capability metadata and omission diagnostics.

category: GET Authorization Plumbing
description: Carry AuthorizationStrategyEvaluators through the backend-local relational GET seam so descriptor GET can fail closed without invoking the legacy ResourceAuthorizationHandler. Populate evaluators from GetByIdHandler on RelationalGetRequest and expose them through IRelationalGetRequest. Do not widen public IGetRequest/GetRequest unless a compile-time seam proves it is necessary. Acceptance: public non-relational GET contracts remain unchanged; relational descriptor GET allows empty strategies or only NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired; any other effective strategy returns GetFailureNotImplemented with a diagnostic naming the resource and strategies; relational descriptor GET does not call the legacy authorization handler; existing non-descriptor GET behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add Core/backend unit tests for evaluator propagation from GetByIdHandler to IRelationalGetRequest and for descriptor GET fail-closed behavior.
Add a unit test proving public IGetRequest consumers are not forced to carry AuthorizationStrategyEvaluators unless the implementation intentionally widens that contract.

category: Descriptor Read Handler Contracts and DI Registration
description: Introduce IDescriptorReadHandler/DescriptorReadHandler as a DI-registered relational read component parallel to IDescriptorWriteHandler, with explicit descriptor read request contracts for GET-by-id and GET-many. The GET request contract should carry MappingSet, descriptor ConcreteResourceModel, qualified resource, DocumentUuid, read mode, authorization evaluators, readable-profile context, and trace id. The query request contract should carry MappingSet, descriptor ConcreteResourceModel, qualified resource, query elements, pagination, authorization evaluators, readable-profile context, and trace id. Acceptance: DI resolves IDescriptorReadHandler with relational backend services; the handler can be constructed without replacing IDescriptorWriteHandler; descriptor GET/query request contracts expose all required execution inputs without widening public IGetRequest/IQueryRequest; non-descriptor read contracts remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add service registration tests showing IDescriptorReadHandler is registered with relational backend services and IDescriptorWriteHandler registration remains unchanged.
Add contract tests proving descriptor GET/query requests carry the mapping set, descriptor model, auth evaluators, read/profile context, pagination/query inputs, and trace id.

category: Descriptor Read Repository Routing and Resource Detection
description: Inject IDescriptorReadHandler into RelationalDocumentStoreRepository and route descriptor GET/query requests to it before generic read-plan, generic GetQueryCapabilityOrThrow, IDocumentHydrator, or IRelationalReadMaterializer paths. Descriptor read routing must use MappingSet.TryGetDescriptorResourceModel(resource, out descriptorModel) rather than ResourceInfo.IsDescriptor alone. Acceptance: SharedDescriptorTable resources route to IDescriptorReadHandler before generic read/query paths; non-descriptor resources continue through existing generic relational read/query paths; descriptor writes and deletes continue to use IDescriptorWriteHandler unchanged; repository constructor/test fixtures are updated without changing public repository behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add repository unit tests proving descriptor GET/query delegation is based on MappingSet SharedDescriptorTable metadata and occurs before generic read-plan/query-capability lookup.
Add repository unit tests proving non-descriptor GET/query still use the generic relational paths and descriptor writes/deletes still use IDescriptorWriteHandler.

category: Descriptor Read Row Contracts and SQL Readers
description: Add descriptor read row contracts and provider-neutral reader helpers for rows returned from dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor. The row shape must include DocumentId, DocumentUuid, ContentLastModifiedAt, ResourceKeyId, Namespace, CodeValue, ShortDescription, Description, EffectiveBeginDate, EffectiveEndDate, and optional diagnostic Discriminator when selected. Acceptance: required descriptor fields namespace, codeValue, and shortDescription being null are classified as invariant/corruption failures; optional null fields are represented as null for later omission; date values round-trip as DateOnly; row readers do not depend on per-descriptor physical tables or project-schema hydration.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for row-reader null handling, DateOnly conversion, required-field invariant failures, optional-field null preservation, and provider column ordinal/name stability.

category: Descriptor GET Handler
description: Implement descriptor GET-by-id in IDescriptorReadHandler. The handler must read only dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor, resolve DocumentUuid to DocumentId, scope by the expected ResourceKeyId, enforce the relational descriptor GET fail-closed authorization rule, and treat dms.Document.ResourceKeyId as authoritative while leaving dms.Descriptor.Discriminator diagnostic-only. Acceptance: missing UUID or UUID for the wrong resource returns GetFailureNotExists; unsupported authorization returns GetFailureNotImplemented before SQL data is returned; matching dms.Document with no dms.Descriptor row returns an UnknownFailure invariant error; a mismatched descriptor Discriminator does not change resource discrimination; no per-descriptor tables, dms.DocumentCache, IDocumentHydrator, IRelationalReadMaterializer, or project-schema reconstitution are used; PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL are parameterized and dialect-quoted consistently.
steps-to-verify: Add repository/handler unit tests for successful descriptor GET delegation, auth fail-closed 501, wrong-resource 404, missing descriptor-row invariant failure, diagnostic-only Discriminator mismatch, and no generic hydrator/materializer invocation for descriptor resources.

category: Descriptor Base Materialization
description: Implement descriptor row-to-JSON materialization before readable-profile projection. ExternalResponse emits namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, effectiveEndDate plus id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate. StoredDocument emits only the public descriptor fields. Optional null fields are omitted, required fields are present, dates use yyyy-MM-dd, _lastModifiedDate uses stored dms.Document ContentLastModifiedAt in UTC second precision, and _etag is base64(SHA-256(canonical descriptor JSON)) for the unprojected external descriptor shape. The canonical descriptor JSON used for _etag removes id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate, recursively orders object members by ordinal string order, preserves array order if any future descriptor shape contains arrays, and serializes minified UTF-8 before hashing. Acceptance: Uri, Discriminator, and ChangeVersion are never emitted; StoredDocument has no id, _etag, _lastModifiedDate, ChangeVersion, or profile projection; descriptor responses are materialized directly from selected descriptor rows and do not read from or write to dms.DocumentCache; this task does not own post-profile _etag recomputation.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for null omission, date formatting, metadata injection, StoredDocument mode, exclusion of Uri/Discriminator/ChangeVersion, and base _etag computation before projection, including proof that id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate are excluded from the hashed canonical JSON.

category: Descriptor Profile Projection
description: Wire readable profile projection for descriptor GET/query responses while preserving descriptor identity fields. Core should provide descriptor identity property names namespace and codeValue in addition to the existing server-generated fields id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate; the descriptor read handler should invoke the existing IReadableProfileProjector only for ExternalResponse mode and recompute _etag after projection. Acceptance: namespace and codeValue survive readable profiles even when descriptor identity is synthetic; id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate survive all readable projections; _etag reflects the projected served descriptor body; StoredDocument reads remain unprojected; generic non-descriptor profile projection behavior is unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for descriptor readable profile projection preserving namespace/codeValue and API metadata while filtering non-identity descriptor fields.
Add Core seam tests showing descriptor read requests pass namespace/codeValue as identity property names when a readable profile applies.

category: Descriptor Query Preprocessing and Predicate Binding
description: Implement descriptor-specific query preprocessing from descriptor query capabilities before SQL planning. Support exact-match predicates for id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate. Query parameter lookup is case-insensitive, id parses as UUID with invalid values returning an empty page, date values follow DMS-993 exact-match empty-page semantics for unrepresentable values, and descriptor string values are passed through without normalization. Acceptance: unsupported or omitted descriptor query capability produces a 501-ready diagnostic; invalid id returns an empty QuerySuccess without executing SQL; strict namespace matching is preserved so ?namespace=<namespace>#<codeValue> only matches if that full value is stored in Namespace; preprocessing emits deterministic predicate bindings for the SQL planner.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for case-insensitive query parameter lookup, all supported descriptor fields, invalid id empty-page behavior, unrepresentable date empty-page behavior, strict namespace value handling, and unsupported/omitted capability diagnostics.

category: Descriptor Query Page SQL Planning
description: Implement descriptor-specific dialect SQL planning for page selection from preprocessed descriptor predicates. The page query must select dms.Document.DocumentId scoped by expected ResourceKeyId, join dms.Descriptor only when descriptor-column predicates are present, order by dms.Document.DocumentId ascending, and apply deterministic offset/limit paging. Acceptance: SQL never targets per-descriptor tables; generated parameter names are stable across PostgreSQL and SQL Server; id predicates target dms.Document.DocumentUuid; descriptor field predicates target dms.Descriptor columns from DescriptorMetadata.ColumnContract; page SQL includes ResourceKeyId discrimination even when there are no descriptor-column filters; string comparisons are case-sensitive/ordinal, including SQL Server where provider default collation may otherwise be case-insensitive.
steps-to-verify: Add unit/golden tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server descriptor page SQL, stable parameter names, id predicate SQL, all public descriptor fields, no per-descriptor table references, and ResourceKeyId discrimination.
Add explicit mixed-case string filter tests proving SQL Server and PostgreSQL page SQL do not match descriptor string values using case-insensitive/provider-default semantics.

category: Descriptor Query Total Count SQL Planning
description: Implement descriptor-specific totalCount SQL planning from the same preprocessed descriptor predicates used by page SQL. The count query must count matching dms.Document rows scoped by expected ResourceKeyId and must apply the same id and descriptor-column filters as the page query without offset/limit paging. Acceptance: totalCount SQL never targets per-descriptor tables; totalCount SQL uses the same ResourceKeyId discrimination and predicate bindings as page SQL; generated parameter names are stable and compatible with the page-query parameter set; SQL Server string predicates use the same binary/case-sensitive comparison behavior as page SQL; PostgreSQL and SQL Server count plans return the unpaged filtered row count.
steps-to-verify: Add unit/golden tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server descriptor totalCount SQL with no filters, id filters, descriptor field filters, and mixed string-case filters.
Add tests proving totalCount SQL and page SQL share the same filtering semantics while count SQL omits offset/limit paging.

category: Descriptor Query Delegation and Guard Rails
description: Add descriptor GET-many entry-point handling to RelationalDocumentStoreRepository and IDescriptorReadHandler before the generic GetQueryCapabilityOrThrow path. Use descriptor query capability lookup, enforce ResourceKeyId discrimination, fail closed for non-no-op authorization, and return an empty QuerySuccess for empty-page preprocessing. Acceptance: omitted descriptor capability returns QueryFailureNotImplemented with the descriptor capability diagnostic; unsupported authorization returns QueryFailureNotImplemented with the resource and strategy names; invalid id preprocessing returns an empty page without executing SQL; generic IDocumentHydrator and project-schema reconstitution are not invoked for descriptor resources.
steps-to-verify: Add repository/handler unit tests for descriptor query delegation, omitted capability 501, auth fail-closed 501, empty-page preprocessing, and no generic hydrator/materializer invocation.

category: Descriptor 501 Response Mapping
description: Verify Core and backend response mapping for descriptor read failures that intentionally return not implemented during the interim authorization/capability rollout. Acceptance: descriptor GET with unsupported effective authorization returns GetFailureNotImplemented and maps to HTTP 501 with an actionable diagnostic; descriptor query with unsupported effective authorization returns QueryFailureNotImplemented and maps to HTTP 501 with the resource and strategy names; descriptor query with an omitted descriptor query capability returns QueryFailureNotImplemented and maps to HTTP 501 with the descriptor capability diagnostic; these intentional 501 cases are not surfaced as UnknownFailure or HTTP 500.
steps-to-verify: Add Core/backend unit tests for descriptor GET auth failure response mapping from GetFailureNotImplemented to HTTP 501.
Add Core/backend unit tests for descriptor query auth failure and omitted descriptor query capability response mapping from QueryFailureNotImplemented to HTTP 501.

category: Descriptor Query Row Retrieval and Invariant Validation
description: Execute descriptor GET-many page and total-count SQL through IDescriptorReadHandler against dms.Document plus dms.Descriptor, returning descriptor row contracts for the selected page. The selected page must be based on dms.Document rows scoped by ResourceKeyId, and descriptor rows must be validated after selection so corruption is not silently inner-joined away. totalCount=true counts the unpaged filtered document set and must not perform a global corruption scan for descriptor documents outside the selected page. Acceptance: totalCount=true is honored; row retrieval returns rows ordered by DocumentId; a selected descriptor document missing a dms.Descriptor row returns a 500-style invariant failure, but a corrupt descriptor document outside the returned page does not fail the request solely because totalCount was requested; required descriptor field nulls return a 500-style invariant failure; no per-descriptor physical tables or dms.DocumentCache are used; this task does not own final JSON/profile materialization.
steps-to-verify: Add handler unit tests for totalCount, ordered row retrieval, selected-document missing descriptor-row invariant failure, corrupt descriptor document outside the selected page not failing solely because totalCount was requested, required-field null invariant failure, and provider-specific command shape.

category: Descriptor Query Page Materialization
description: Materialize descriptor GET-many rows into response items directly from descriptor row contracts. Apply ExternalResponse descriptor materialization, readable profile projection when present, and post-projection _etag recomputation per served item. Acceptance: returned items are ordered by DocumentId; optional null descriptor fields are omitted; Uri, Discriminator, and ChangeVersion are never emitted; profile-projected query items preserve namespace, codeValue, id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate; no project-schema hydration, generic reconstitution, or dms.DocumentCache participation is used.
steps-to-verify: Add handler/materialization unit tests for ordered query items, null optional-field omission, metadata shape, profile-projected query items, post-projection _etag recomputation, and exclusion of Uri/Discriminator/ChangeVersion.

category: Provider Integration Test Infrastructure
description: Add shared PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration-test helpers for directly seeding dms.Document and dms.Descriptor rows and for resolving descriptor ResourceKeyId values from the MappingSet. Keep these helpers scoped to descriptor read tests and avoid depending on descriptor POST unless a test explicitly covers write/read integration. Acceptance: both providers can seed valid descriptor rows, wrong-resource document rows, mixed-case descriptor values, and corrupt missing-descriptor-row cases deterministically; helpers preserve dms schema metadata; tests can request ExternalResponse and StoredDocument modes through RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.
steps-to-verify: Add provider helper tests or smoke tests proving seeded rows can be read by raw SQL before repository assertions run.

category: Provider Integration GET and Invariant Tests
description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration tests for descriptor GET behavior using direct dms.Document/dms.Descriptor seeding. Cover GET by id, StoredDocument mode, wrong-resource 404, auth fail-closed 501, diagnostic-only Discriminator mismatch, selected-document missing descriptor-row invariant failure, null optional-field omission, metadata shape, _lastModifiedDate, and exclusion of Uri/Discriminator/ChangeVersion. Acceptance: both providers pass the same GET/invariant matrix and prove descriptor reads use the shared descriptor table rather than per-descriptor physical tables.

category: Provider Integration Query Filter Tests
description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration tests for descriptor query filters using direct dms.Document/dms.Descriptor seeding. Cover id, namespace, codeValue, shortDescription, description, effectiveBeginDate, and effectiveEndDate. Include invalid id empty-page behavior, strict namespace matching, and mixed-case string values. Acceptance: both providers pass the same filter matrix; descriptor string filters are case-sensitive/ordinal and do not inherit SQL Server default case-insensitive matching; ?namespace=<namespace>#<codeValue> only matches when that full value is stored in Namespace; descriptor query SQL is provider-correct for UUID and date behavior.

category: Provider Integration Query Paging, Count, and Invariant Tests
description: Add PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration tests for descriptor query paging, totalCount, and query-time invariant failures using direct dms.Document/dms.Descriptor seeding. Cover deterministic ordering by DocumentId, paging across at least two pages, totalCount=true with and without filters, empty-page preprocessing that avoids SQL execution when observable, and a selected dms.Document descriptor row missing dms.Descriptor. Acceptance: both providers pass the same paging/count matrix; query pages are stable for a fixed committed state; total count matches the unpaged filtered row count; selected corrupt descriptor documents return a 500-style invariant failure rather than being silently skipped.

category: Relational Descriptor API E2E
description: Switch any legacy E2Es to relational-backend that creates a descriptor through POST, reads it by id, and queries it by descriptor fields. Add one thin relational-backend E2E scenario that creates a descriptor through POST, reads it by id, queries it by descriptor fields, and exercises readable profile projection through the public API. Prefer tagging existing descriptor GET/query scenarios with relational-backend when their expectations already match the relational design; add a focused scenario only where legacy expectations conflict. The task must identify an existing readable-profile fixture that targets a descriptor resource or add the minimal descriptor-readable-profile fixture needed for the scenario. Acceptance: the scenario(s) proves Core routing, descriptor write/read integration, descriptor query routing, profile projection, _etag recomputation, _lastModifiedDate, and correct public descriptor shape without Uri or Discriminator; namespace, codeValue, id, _etag, and _lastModifiedDate survive readable profile projection; deeper provider/filter/invariant coverage and fail-closed authorization coverage remain in backend/unit tests.

category: Formatting and Regression Sweep
description: Run the final formatting and regression sweep for DMS-994 after the implementation tasks land. Acceptance: modified C# files are csharpier-formatted without unrelated directory-wide churn, descriptor read/query unit and integration tests pass, generic relational query/read tests still pass, descriptor query capability manifest/golden tests pass, and any intentional legacy descriptor-query expectation changes are documented in test names or comments.
steps-to-verify: Run: dotnet csharpier format <modified C# files or the smallest modified directories>
Run the PostgreSQL and SQL Server descriptor read integration tests from the provider integration tasks.
Run the relational descriptor E2E filter from the Relational Descriptor API E2E task.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor E2E ETag Assertion
description: Fix the relational descriptor readable-profile E2E scenario so it stores the descriptor _etag from the GET response body instead of the etag response header. Acceptance: the scenario no longer depends on a GET response header that GetByIdHandler does not emit; the stored value comes from the JSON body path _etag; the scenario still verifies readable profile projection and descriptor _etag behavior through the public API.
steps-to-verify: Update src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Descriptors/ReadDescriptorsValidation.feature to store the GET response body _etag rather than the etag header.
Confirm the step helper used by the scenario does not hard-fail on a missing GET etag header.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor Query Schema Collision Handling
description: Prevent descriptor query ApiSchema query-field collisions from failing the whole MappingSet through the generic relational query compiler. Acceptance: descriptor resources with queryFieldMapping disagreements are handled by DescriptorQueryCapabilityCompiler as resource-scoped omitted descriptor query capabilities; MappingSet compilation succeeds for unaffected resources; SharedDescriptorTable resources still receive the generic RelationalQueryCapability descriptor-resource omission metadata after skipping generic collision validation; generic RelationalQueryCapability behavior for non-descriptor resources remains unchanged; diagnostics clearly identify the affected descriptor resource and mismatch.
steps-to-verify: Adjust MappingSet compilation so SharedDescriptorTable resources do not run generic query-field collision validation before descriptor-specific omission handling can occur.
Add or update unit tests where a descriptor resource has a case-insensitive query-field collision or descriptor query schema mismatch and only that descriptor query capability is omitted.
Add or update unit tests proving the same collision behavior still fails or omits according to the existing generic rules for non-descriptor resources.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor Authorization Guard Simplification
description: Remove duplicated descriptor GET/query authorization fail-closed logic between RelationalDocumentStoreRepository and DescriptorReadHandler. Acceptance: descriptor authorization is checked in one place; descriptor GET and query both return descriptor-specific 501 diagnostics naming the resource and unsupported strategies; repository routing still delegates descriptor reads before generic read/query paths; non-descriptor authorization behavior is unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Centralize descriptor GET/query authorization fail-closed handling in DescriptorReadHandler or another single shared helper.
Remove the duplicate repository guard or duplicate handler guard, keeping descriptor query diagnostics consistent.
Add or update unit tests for descriptor GET and descriptor query unsupported authorization to assert the final 501 diagnostic text.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor Read Contract Dead Code
description: Remove the unused DescriptorResourceModel payload from descriptor GET/query request contracts, or make the handler use it as the authoritative descriptor metadata source. Acceptance: DescriptorReadHandler request contracts carry only data used by the handler; repository descriptor detection still uses MappingSet.TryGetDescriptorResourceModel; tests no longer assert inert DescriptorResourceModel round-tripping; descriptor GET/query behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Remove DescriptorResourceModel from DescriptorGetByIdRequest and DescriptorQueryRequest unless the handler is changed to consume it directly.
Update repository construction of descriptor read requests and associated contract/unit tests.
Search for DescriptorResourceModel request-property usages and confirm no dead pass-through remains.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor Id Query SQL Simplification
description: Simplify descriptor ?id= query SQL so it filters the descriptor page root dms.Document row directly instead of adding a redundant self-join to dms.Document. Acceptance: descriptor page SQL remains scoped by ResourceKeyId and ordered by DocumentId; id predicates filter r.DocumentUuid directly or use an equivalent no-self-join plan; totalCount=true count SQL uses the same no-self-join id filtering; descriptor-column filters still join dms.Descriptor only when needed; PostgreSQL and SQL Server generated SQL remain parameterized and deterministic.
steps-to-verify: Update DescriptorQueryPageKeysetPlanner or PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler so descriptor id predicates do not add an extra dms.Document join when the root table is already dms.Document.
Add or update PostgreSQL and SQL Server SQL planner tests proving descriptor id query page and totalCount SQL have no redundant dms.Document self-join.

category: Review Follow-up: Descriptor Read/Query Diagnostic Text
description: Update stale descriptor read/query omission diagnostics that still refer to E08-S05 as a future story. Acceptance: diagnostics no longer imply descriptor read or query support is unimplemented by this story; generic descriptor root-table query omission still explains that descriptor resources use descriptor-specific query capability metadata; descriptor read-plan omission still explains that descriptor resources use the descriptor read path; manifest/golden outputs are updated consistently.
steps-to-verify: Update RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler and MappingSetLookupExtensions descriptor omission text to remove stale future-story references.
Update affected manifest/golden snapshots or unit test expectations.
